domain_knowledge: ENPKG is a sample-centric computational framework designed to integrate spectral data, molecular networking, and knowledge-graph outputs to support natural products drug discovery.
The workflow expects raw input data organized in a specific directory structure; correct file placement and naming conventions are critical for successful execution.
The complete pipeline encompasses feature detection, spectral library matching, and knowledge-graph triple generation as sequential but interconnected steps.
Output validation requires verification that all intermediate and final files conform to expected schemas and contain non-empty annotated results.

direct_checks: verify that the enpkg_full repository (github:enpkg__enpkg_full) is accessible and contains executable workflow code
verify file_exists for a test or example dataset directory within the repository
script_runs: execute the ENPKG full workflow against the provided example/test dataset without errors
file_exists: verify that expected annotated output files are generated in the designated output directory after workflow completion
file_format_is: verify that output files conform to knowledge-graph-ready formats (e.g., GraphML, JSON, TSV, or other structured formats documented in the repository)
output_matches_reference: compare generated outputs against reference outputs (if provided in the repository) byte-for-byte or robust to minor formatting variations depending on output specification
expert_review: evaluate whether the generated knowledge graph outputs are scientifically coherent and contain expected natural products annotations (structures, metadata, relationships)
assess whether the workflow execution demonstrates successful integration of all stated pipeline components (sample annotation, chemical annotation, network inference)

methodology_summary: Install ENPKG full workflow from repository and configure the computational environment with all required dependencies.
Prepare and validate input dataset (spectral data and sample metadata) in the workflow's expected directory structure.
Execute the ENPKG orchestrator, which automatically chains feature detection, spectral matching, molecular networking, and knowledge-graph annotation.
Monitor execution logs and verify successful completion of all pipeline stages.

Validation: Confirm that all expected output files (annotated features, knowledge-graph triples, spectral matches) exist, are non-empty, conform to output schema, and contain molecular annotations with confidence scores for test dataset samples.

title: Reconstruct the end-to-end ENPKG pipeline execution on a sample dataset
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Clone or obtain the enpkg_full repository from github.com/enpkg/enpkg_full and install all dependencies as specified in the setup documentation. 2. Prepare the example or test dataset by placing raw input files (spectral data, sample metadata) in the designated input directory according to the workflow's expected directory structure. 3. Run the ENPKG full workflow orchestrator, which automatically executes the complete computational pipeline from raw data ingestion through feature detection, molecular networking, and knowledge-graph annotation. 4. Monitor workflow execution for successful completion of all intermediate steps and absence of critical errors. 5. Validate the final annotated output files (molecular annotations, spectral matches, knowledge-graph triples) against the expected output schema and integrity checks.
